creator: romain@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to Dima Panov from comment #7)
> Try this: https://people.freebsd.org/~fluffy/patches/ruby.diff

I have just applied this patch and build/installed lang/ruby24, and I confirm that I can't reproduce the problem: everything seems to be working as expected.

Because I really don't understand what this patch is supposed to do, the facts: building with an without the patch is always fine: only runtime happen to produce errors without your patch.  So maybe it's not enough to do an exp-run if the built program/libraries are never used in some way?  That might explain some undetected failures.  Running `make test` on these ports fails without the above patch:
  - devel/leatherman
  - sysutils/facter
